name: Incline Dumbbell Press
resistance_profile: Incline (~30 degrees) biases the clavicular (upper) chest; dumbbells allow a deeper stretch at the bottom than a barbell.
rom_notes: Lower until a comfortable deep stretch across the upper chest; do not let the shoulders roll forward.
cues: Lower under control into the stretch
Press the dumbbells up and slightly together
execution_steps: Set the bench to a low incline (about 15-30 degrees) and sit with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back and kick the dumbbells up to arm's length over your upper chest.
Lower under control into a deep stretch across the upper chest, elbows about 45 degrees from the torso.
Press the dumbbells up and slightly together, keeping them stacked over the upper chest.
good_when: You want upper-chest emphasis with a deeper stretch than an incline barbell.
You want an even left-right load and a shoulder-friendly path.
bad_when: The incline is set too steep and it turns into a shoulder press.
You want to move maximal absolute load (an incline barbell allows more).
common_errors: Incline set too steep (turns into a shoulder press)
Short range at the top only
progressions: Add a pause in the stretched position
